description: Introduce narrow token_info education organization lookup contracts and move GetTokenInfoHandler off direct IAuthorizationRepository usage for education organization response data.
acceptance-criteria: Token_info-specific lookup contracts exist for default and relational education organization response rows, with the relational contract receiving the request MappingSet.
GetTokenInfoHandler selects the relational lookup when it is registered, otherwise selects the default lookup, and does not depend directly on IAuthorizationRepository.
The existing token_info response contract is preserved, including claim_set, education_organizations, namespace_prefixes, resources, services, and assigned profiles.
Tokens with no education organization claims return a 200 response with an empty education_organizations array without invoking an education organization lookup.
Normal resource authorization paths are not changed by this boundary split.
steps-to-verify: Review GetTokenInfoHandler, ITokenInfoEducationOrganizationLookup, and IRelationalTokenInfoEducationOrganizationLookup to confirm token_info uses narrow lookup services for education organization rows.
Review the IAuthorizationRepository registration and confirm it remains available for legacy/non-relational authorization behavior.

description: Add the default token_info lookup adapter that delegates to the existing IAuthorizationRepository.GetTokenInfoEducationOrganizations implementation.
acceptance-criteria: The non-relational/default path returns the same TokenInfoEducationOrganization rows as the existing legacy repository method.
The adapter does not alter education organization IDs, names, discriminators, ancestor discriminators, or ancestor IDs.
Existing non-relational token_info behavior remains unchanged.
The adapter is registered only as the default token_info lookup and does not replace IAuthorizationRepository.
steps-to-verify: Review the adapter implementation and service registration for the non-relational/default path.

description: Build the cross-dialect relational token_info education organization SQL plan from the runtime mapping set.
acceptance-criteria: The plan derives a concrete EducationOrganization projection from the mapping set's EducationOrganization abstract union members.
The projection selects DocumentId, EducationOrganizationId, NameOfInstitution, and relational discriminator from concrete relational root tables.
NameOfInstitution is resolved from concrete resource root scalar metadata rather than by expanding the abstract EducationOrganization view contract.
The plan uses auth.EducationOrganizationIdToEducationOrganizationId for reachable education organizations and ancestors.
The plan adds direct self rows for claimed education organization IDs that resolve to concrete education organization rows.
The plan never references dms.EducationOrganizationHierarchy or other legacy document-store-only hierarchy tables.
Empty claim lists, unresolved claimed IDs, and stale auth hierarchy tuples return no rows or omit only affected ancestor fields rather than failing token_info.
steps-to-verify: Review the SQL compiler output for both PostgreSQL and SQL Server dialects.
Search the relational token_info implementation for dms.EducationOrganizationHierarchy and confirm it is not referenced.

description: Implement the PostgreSQL relational token_info education organization lookup service and parameter binding.
acceptance-criteria: When relational PostgreSQL is active, the lookup executes the relational SQL plan against the request database connection.
Claimed education organization IDs are bound using PostgreSQL-safe parameters.
Rows are mapped to TokenInfoEducationOrganization with education organization ID, name of institution, discriminator, ancestor discriminator, and ancestor education organization ID.
The lookup returns an empty result for empty or fully unresolved claim lists without throwing.
The lookup depends on the relational auth table and concrete education organization tables, not legacy document-store hierarchy tables.
steps-to-verify: Review the PostgreSQL lookup implementation and row reader.

description: Implement the SQL Server relational token_info education organization lookup service and parameter binding.
acceptance-criteria: When relational SQL Server is active, the lookup executes the relational SQL plan against the request database connection.
Claimed education organization IDs are bound using SQL Server-safe parameterization.
Rows are mapped to TokenInfoEducationOrganization with the same projection contract as PostgreSQL.
The SQL Server implementation handles empty and fully unresolved claim lists without throwing.
Provider-backed SQL Server coverage executes the generated query against a real SQL Server database.
steps-to-verify: Review the SQL Server lookup implementation and parameter binding.

With ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin set to a running SQL Server, run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~MssqlTokenInfoEducationOrganizationLookup"

description: Register token_info lookup implementations according to datastore and AppSettings__UseRelationalBackend while preserving legacy authorization registrations.
acceptance-criteria: When UseRelationalBackend is false, token_info uses the legacy adapter and no relational token_info lookup is selected.
When UseRelationalBackend is true with PostgreSQL, the PostgreSQL relational token_info lookup is available for GetTokenInfoHandler.
When UseRelationalBackend is true with SQL Server, the SQL Server relational token_info lookup is available for GetTokenInfoHandler.
The existing IAuthorizationRepository registration is not replaced by the relational token_info lookup.
The token_info path resolves the request MappingSet before invoking a relational lookup and stops before lookup when mapping-set resolution fails.
steps-to-verify: Review WebApplicationBuilderExtensions and provider service collection extension registrations.

description: Format relational education organization discriminators and ancestor fields into the existing token_info response shape.
acceptance-criteria: Standard Ed-Fi discriminators such as Ed-Fi:School render type values such as edfi.School.
Ancestor ID property names are derived from ResourceName and converted to the existing snake_case token_info names such as school_id and local_education_agency_id.
Extension or non-Ed-Fi project names resolve through ApiSchemaDocuments to projectEndpointName for type values.
Extension or non-Ed-Fi ancestor property names still derive from ResourceName, not projectEndpointName.
Unresolvable discriminator project names do not silently emit malformed token_info type values.
steps-to-verify: Review GetTokenInfoHandler discriminator parsing and formatting helpers.
Confirm focused unit coverage includes extension or non-Ed-Fi discriminator rendering with a synthetic schema.

description: Add unit and provider integration coverage for relational token_info lookup edge cases and cross-dialect parity.
acceptance-criteria: Unit tests prove SQL generation includes concrete education organization projection arms, auth.EducationOrganizationIdToEducationOrganizationId joins, direct claim rows, and no legacy hierarchy table references.
Unit tests cover empty claim lists, unresolved claimed education organization IDs, stale target rows, and missing ancestor projection rows.
PostgreSQL provider integration tests execute the relational lookup against real relational tables and auth data.
SQL Server provider integration tests execute the relational lookup against real relational tables and auth data.
Tests verify the same projection contract across PostgreSQL and SQL Server.
steps-to-verify: With ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin set to a running SQL Server, run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~TokenInfoEducationOrganizationLookup"

description: Use the existing token introspection E2E scenario as the PostgreSQL relational backend signal for the full endpoint path.
acceptance-criteria: The existing happy-path token_info scenario is tagged with relational-backend and an appropriate relational CI shard tag.
The E2E scenario uses real CMS application and token wiring.
The E2E scenario validates the hierarchy-shaped education_organizations response with education organization ID, name, type, and ancestor fields.
The relational E2E run returns 200 and does not fail with PostgreSQL 42P01 for dms.EducationOrganizationHierarchy.
No duplicate relational-only happy-path E2E scenario is added for the same behavior.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/Security/TokenIntrospection.feature for relational-backend tagging on scenario 01.

From the repository root, run ./build-dms.ps1 E2ETest -Configuration Release -SkipDockerBuild -IdentityProvider self-contained -EnvironmentFile './.env.e2e.relational' -TestFilter 'Category=@relational-backend&Category=@relational-ci-shard-3'
If the E2E run fails before issuing API requests, inspect the dms-local Docker logs and environment setup before treating it as a product failure.

description: Format and run the focused test suite needed to validate the token_info relational lookup story without broad unrelated refactoring.
acceptance-criteria: All modified C# files are formatted with csharpier.
Focused unit tests for core handler behavior, service registration, SQL planning, and provider lookup wrappers pass.
PostgreSQL relational integration coverage passes.
SQL Server relational integration coverage passes when a SQL Server admin connection string is available.
The PostgreSQL relational E2E shard containing token_info passes or any environment-only failure is documented with Docker/runtime evidence.
steps-to-verify: With ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin set to a running SQL Server, run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~TokenInfoEducationOrganizationLookup"
